todaysEvents.summary: US strikes Iran's Qeshm Island; Iran drone hits Kuwait airport — 1 killed, flights suspended (Jun 3, Al Jazeera)
todaysEvents.delta: Up: US attacked Iran's Qeshm Island — strategic island near Hormuz, IRGC naval base (Al Jazeera Jun 3). Iran retaliated with drone strike on Kuwait International Airport: fuel tanks hit, 1 killed, several wounded, flights suspended. Iran also targeted Bahrain. Rubio: US will lift sanctions only if Iran gives up enriched uranium — rejecting Hormuz-linked deal. Iran FM: 'ceasefire on all fronts, including in Lebanon' — breach on one front = breach on all. Escalation from Jun 1-2 military exchange to strategic asset strikes. Brent third consecutive up session to $98.57 (TradingView Jun 3). Tail raised to 42%.

todaysEvents.summary: Rubio rejects Hormuz-linked deal: sanctions only lifted if Iran gives up enriched uranium (Jun 3, Al Jazeera)
todaysEvents.delta: Up: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio says the US will lift sanctions on Iran only if it gives up enriched uranium — explicitly rejecting a Hormuz-linked deal that would have separated Strait reopening from nuclear negotiations (Al Jazeera Jun 3). Iran insists on 'ceasefire on all fronts including Lebanon.' MOU framework now in serious jeopardy — prior 'largely negotiated' Phase 1 framework (D82) appears dead. Talks suspension (D91) + military escalation (D91-92) + Rubio rejection (D93) = triple blow to deal pathway.

todaysEvents.summary: Brent third consecutive up session: $98.57 (+4.3%) on Qeshm strike, Kuwait airport attack (Jun 3, TradingView)
todaysEvents.delta: Up: Brent $98.57 (TradingView Jun 3, +4.3% from Jun 2 close $94.53). Third consecutive up session — erasing most of May's -19% decline. WTI $96.25 (+5.0%). TTF €49.50 (+11.2%, TradingView). Risk-off across EM: iTraxx Asia IG ~195bp (+13bp), ASEAN HY ~580bp (+25bp). EM FX weaker across board. Energy equities +1.6%, shipping +2.4%, banks -2.1%. Tail scenario probability raised to 42% on Qeshm strike + airport attack + Rubio rejection of Hormuz deal.

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Triple blow to deal pathway: (1) Iran suspended peace talks D91; (2) US-Iran direct military exchange D91-92; (3) US strikes Qeshm Island, Iran hits Kuwait airport, Rubio rejects Hormuz-linked deal D93. Israel-Hezbollah halt of Jun 1 appears broken — Israel escalates Lebanon attacks. MOU 'largely negotiated' framework (D82) now appears dead — Rubio explicitly requiring enriched uranium concession before sanctions relief. Base case retained only as residual: remote chance talks resume via backchannel with significant concessions from either side. If against all odds: Brent retreats toward $80-85/bbl; P&I restoration enables commercial traffic; Ras Laffan restart ~late Aug 2026.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Diplomacy severely damaged but not entirely dead. Rubio left theoretical door open for 'right deal'. Iran warns Lebanon breach = all fronts breach — tying fronts together complicates de-escalation. Trump 'relax' comment suggests US may not seek full war escalation. Military exchange continues at elevated tempo but neither side seeks full theatre war. Stalemate: Hormuz ~90% closed, P&I suspended, mine-clearing ~6 months, Ras Laffan restart late Aug 2026 at earliest. Brent stabilises $95-110/bbl; TTF €47-53/MWh; JKM $17-21/MMBtu; iTraxx 185-210bp. Dominant scenario (48%) — active conflict but contained.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Qeshm Island strike + Kuwait airport attack + Rubio rejection = three escalatory steps in one day. Iran ties Lebanon/Gaza breaches to all-fronts ceasefire — any continued Israel attacks on Lebanon are grounds for Iran to break off completely. Netanyahu faces Israeli backlash over Lebanon strategy (Al Jazeera Jun 3) — may escalate to maintain domestic support. Full theatre escalation: Kharg Island seizure or GCC production targeting; Brent spikes $125-165/bbl; Malaysia July-August supply crisis; iTraxx spikes to 230-260bp. Tail raised from 35% to 42% on D93 triple escalation.
